observations.recommendation:

Likewise, ensure that the whereabouts, conditions and other relevant information of each PPE item are properly provided in the “Remarks” column of the RPCPPE for proper accountability of PPEs of the LGU.

Further, we have recommended that a copy of PAR be maintained by the Property Officer to ensure that accountability over the issued PPE is enforced prior to separation from service.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the LGU of Villasis, being a first-class municipality with PPEs and inventories in huge amounts and quantities, consider the creation of a plantilla position for GSO to perform functions provided by laws, rules and regulations for the advancement of the LGU, where such functions also include the following:

To accept deliveries of properties and supplies and issue accordingly with PAR, ICS, or RIS to end-users, as the case may be.

To prepare and maintain records and reports of properties and supplies for proper control, monitoring, and accountability.

To spearhead the conduct of the One-time Cleansing of PPEs, which includes proper disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

To maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008, dated November 23, 2015.
observations.managementAction: Management had instructed the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare an action plan where different strategies could be applied based on the age and nature of the collectibles and realistically project collections under the circumstances; and 

Prepare an aging schedule of delinquent accounts for monitoring purposes and for determination of a five-year collection period from the date taxes become due.
observations.managementAction: Management had instructed the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason:
Management continuously maintained a high collection efficiency rate in CY 2024.

No aging was provided to the Audit Team

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the appropriate adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts to bring the communication equipment's net book value to its correct balance.

We have recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to adopt the accrual method for recognizing income.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry voucher to take up the unrecorded Operating Lease Receivable and the corresponding income accounts from uncollected stall rentals pursuant to Paragraph 9 of IPSAS 9.
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Market Supervisor, to enforce the collection of delinquent accounts. Should there be any resistance to payment, we recommend that the lease contract be cancelled or revoked, and that the lease be re-assigned to a compliant stallholder or readvertised to the public. Nevertheless, the previous stallholders will remain liable for the unpaid rentals.
Require the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Market Supervisor, to update the Certified List of Delinquent Stallholders, including the corresponding amounts of surcharges and interest, in accordance with the provisions of the Updated Villasis Market Code of 2022.

In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all semi-expendable properties acquired and issued before CY 2024 with a total carrying amount of  ₱916,387.17, are properly adjusted/corrected in the books of accounts in accordance with Annex C of COA Circular 2024-006, which provides illustrative accounting entries to ensure proper recording, adjustments, and reclassification of accounts relative to the increase of the capitalization threshold of PPEs.
Similarly, we have recommended that the accountability forms mentioned above be maintained to ensure that the transfer of PPEs to semi-expendable expenses is accompanied by measures that uphold accountability.

In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Card (SPLC); and
Instruct the designated Supply/Property Officer to prepare and maintain the forms, registries, and reports prescribed under Item 4.7 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022, to enhance accountability and internal control over semi-expendable property. Preparing and maintaining the prescribed forms, records, and reports will ensure accurate recording, effective monitoring, and proper accountability of semi-expendable property, thereby minimizing the risk of loss, unauthorized use, or potential misuse.

We have reiterated our recommendation from the previous year that Management determine and adopt a uniform property identification system for PPE, two to three months after this date, wherein a unique property number shall be assigned for each item. Likewise, ensure that the whereabouts, conditions and other relevant information of each PPE item are properly provided in the “Remarks” column of the RPCPPE for proper accountability of PPEs of the LGU.
Moreover, it was recommended that the original copy of the PAR be retained by the individual responsible for issuing property and equipment to end-users. This helps maintain accountability over the issued PPEs in case of loss or misuse of LGU property, and facilitates the return of the property before the end-user separates from the service.

To address the deficiencies identified in the internal controls regarding the granting and liquidation of cash advances, we have recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to perform the following actions:
Refrain from granting cash advances that exceed the allowable maximum cash accountability covered by the fidelity bond of the accountable officer unless the amount of the bond has been increased in accordance with the schedule of cash accountability and the bond issued by the Bureau of the Treasury pursuant to Annex C of Treasury Circular No. 02-2019;
Issue a certification among the additional documents for cash advance transactions, confirming that the official or employee requesting the cash advance has no outstanding cash advances; and
Monitor the liquidation of cash advances and ensure that they are liquidated as soon as the purpose for which they were granted has been served.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Market Supervisor, to cause the renewal of the Contract of Lease that had expired and were deemed expired in order to protect the rights/interest of the local government unit and to enforce compliance from the stallholders. This is without prejudice to the intention of the LGU to install other means of internal control to ensure that those tenants with the intention to renew their contracts do not breach/violate the terms and conditions stipulated in the previous agreements.

We have recommended that Management:
Submit a formal justification for implementing the Installation/Rehabilitation of Solar Street Lights (Municipal Wide) Project;
Avoid undertaking projects that fall outside legal provisions, as this could result in unlawful expenditures; and
Ensure that the shares from excise tax are exclusively used for programs and projects listed under Section 8 of RA No. 1035. Adhering to legal provisions is essential to guarantee transparency, accountability, and the appropriate use of government funds.

We recommended that Management require the BAC and Technical Working Group:
To justify awarding the contract to Golden Leaf Trading Corporation, given that the eligibility requirements submitted were not complete;
To scrutinize the eligibility documents of the bidders as outlined in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that all requirements, including the submission of the NFCC or its equivalent, are strictly enforced; and
To immediately disqualify the bidder who failed to present the documents required for each stage of the procurement process.

In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to purchase cash tickets in ₱5.00 denomination  for use in the Public Market;
Consider hiring the informal laborers as Job Orders of the LGU to be assigned in the collection of fees and maintenance of public restrooms; and
Instruct the Market Supervisor to maintain a daily record of collections and remittances and ensure that collections are remitted daily and intact to the Municipal Treasury Office.

We have recommended that Management provide documents showing that the custody and control of the breeding stocks were not under the LGU but under the 5th District of Pangasinan Dairy Multi-Purpose Cooperative located at Laoac, Pangasinan. Such documents will support the derecognition of the biological assets account to ensure proper presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We have further recommended that, henceforth, any transaction requiring the transfer of properties to other entities whose procurement is implemented by the LGU be supported by an agreement or contract that specifically define the contracting party's accountability and responsibility for the properties involved.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all payroll disbursements for elected officials and regular employees shall be made through the ATM payroll system. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adopt the ATM or digitized payroll system in the disbursement of payroll for salaries and wages of the casuals and JO workers of the LGU. The adoption of a digitized payroll system is highly encouraged because it can improve the cash management practices of the municipality and thereby lessen the risks of possible loss of government funds through misappropriation, theft, or robbery.

Instruct the concerned Job Order personnel to submit justification on the noted unusual attendance as shown in the submitted biometric logs for the information/reference of the Management and further review and evaluation of the Audit Team; and

Enjoin the HRMO, as well as the department heads of various offices of the LGU with assigned Job Order personnel, to strictly monitor the attendance in order to avoid the occurrence of unusual time of log in/out in the biometric system which raises suspicion on the actual rendition of work and validity of the payment.

We have recommended that the payment for Representation Allowance and Transportation Allowance (RATA) be made in accordance with the computation based on the actual work performance as stated in Section 7.1 of Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 in order to avoid excessive payment of the said allowance. 

Submit the Certification/ Attendance duly certified by the HRMO showing the number of workdays of actual performance in a month of LGU Officials entitled to receive RATA be attached as a supporting document prior to disbursement of funds and the amount of P4,500.00 representing excess payment be refunded immediately.

Also COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 requires the same documents as supporting papers for payments for infrastructure projects which is in line with the provisions of Section 4, paragraph 6 of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445 which states that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation.
The non-submission of these pertinent documents caused delay in the verification of the reported accomplishments of the agency and casted doubts on the propriety and validity of the claims.
We issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-029 dated September 11, 2017, AOM No. 2017-030 dated October 24, 2017, AOM No. 2017-031 dated October 24, 2017, AOM No. 2018-005 dated January 11, 2018, AOM No. 2017-032 dated November 6, 2017, AOM No. 2017-033 dated November 6, 2017 and AOM No. 2018-025 dated February 8, 2018, to inform management of our audit observations which we have discussed in the exit conference. During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant was able to submit some of the required documents and had requested for ample time to comply with the given recommendations.
We have recommended that management require the concerned officials and employees submit the supporting documents being required for further evaluation of the COA ROI Technical Evaluation Services and Audit Team and in order to avoid suspension in audit.
We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should ensure the completeness of supporting documents before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001.

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure the completeness of supporting documents before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that management furnish the Office of the Auditor the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity in compliance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-04 and in order for us to validate the infrastructure projects on time.

We have recommended that –
The Bids and Awards Committee refrain from specifying brand names in the preparation of Purchase Requests or Request for Quotations except in cases allowed by the procurement law; and
The contract price strictly adhere with the quotations set forth in the Abstract of Quotations/Canvass corresponding the single or lowest calculated and responsive quotation.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee conduct pre-bid conference be conducted for contracts to be bid with an approved budget of one million pesos (P1,000,000.00) or more in compliance with the provisions of Section 22 of Republic Act 9184.

We have recommended that management strictly use the local funds for its specified purpose as authorized in the appropriation ordinance/ approved budget or seek the authorization of the local sanggunian for the transfer of budget allocated for an approved project/program/activity to another project/program/activity in compliance with Section 305 of RA 7160 and in order to avoid disallowance in audit.

In view of the failure of management to submit legal basis on the payment of financial assistance totalling P54,000.00 and incentives totaling P208,000.00, we have recommended that the concerned officials and employees refund immediately the amounts received. We have further recommended that management ensure that all payments of allowances, incentives, and other benefits to all officials and employees of the local government unit be based on and conform strictly with laws, rules and regulations granting or authorizing such payments.

We have recommended that management observe the rules and regulations on the granting of year-end bonus. Recipients of the year-end incentives herein mentioned should be required to refund the amounts received.

In view of the failure of management to submit legal basis on the payment of the year-end incentives totaling P661,500.00 to various school officials, principals and teachers, we have recommended that management require the concerned DEPED personnel to refund the amounts received. We have further recommended that management ensure that all payments of allowances, incentives, and other benefits be based on and conform strictly with laws, rules and regulations granting or authorizing such payments.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Head of the Local School Board, adhere strictly with the existing laws, rules and regulations in the prioritization and utilization of Special Education Fund particularly for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals and sports development as provided in Sections 100(c) and 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2017 in order to ensure an effective educational development within the locality.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned employees to submit immediately the Liquidation Report and supporting documents covering the utilization of the cash advances amounting to P15,000,000.00 in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and also to avoid the penalties imposed by the existing laws, rules and regulations for non-compliance thereof.

We have reiterated our previous years’ audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact his collections with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations to avoid the risk of loss or improper utilization of government funds. Strict compliance with the provisions of existing laws, rules and regulations in the proper handling, custody and disposition of cash was further recommended.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should record the transactions daily in the cashbooks, balance, rule and close the cashbooks at the end of the day and certify as to the balances at the end of the month in compliance with Section 26 and 27, Volume I of MNGAs and Section 174 of GAAM Volume I to be able to determine his accountability immediately as of the cut-off date or reporting date, as the case may be. Further, he should reconcile the cashbook balance with the cash on hand daily and reconcile his records with the subsidiary ledger balances at least at the end of each quarter to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the cash balances.

We have recommended that the local government unit review their functions, systems and procedures, organizational structure and staffing to determine the appropriate manpower complement for their programs/projects/activities and avoid the hiring of excessive job order workers and save more funds for other services that would benefit the constituents.

We have reiterated our previous years’ audit recommendation that management strictly comply with the provisions of Executive Order No. 298 on the payment of travel expenses to avoid excessive payments of claims for travel expenses. Payments for reimbursement of actual travel expenses in excess of the travel expenses authorized by the same Executive Order should be supported with the required documents in order to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that management ensure that the projects/programs/activities designed under the 20% Development Fund are implemented fully within the timeframe. We have recommended that management require the concerned members of the Bids and Awards Committee, Technical Working Group and BAC Secretariat to refund the honoraria received which was paid out of the 20% Development Fund and utilize the same for other development projects of the agency. All unexpended balances of the 20% Development Fund should be used for development projects, program and activities in compliance with the provisions of Section 287 of Republic Act 7160 and Section 4.0 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all of the infrastructure projects were implemented within the timeframe and require the employees concerned to faithfully supervise and monitor the construction activities, including but not limited to the inspection of works to ensure that the construction is completed as planned in order to deliver timely the essential benefits of the projects to constituents.

We have recommended that the management should adequately handle and account or safeguard the used forms and scaffoldings that were utilized in the construction of infrastructure projects or agency assets. Those that are still in good condition should be saved, preserved and reused in the subsequent construction projects in order to save more funds for programs/projects/activities geared towards the welfare of its constituents.
We have further recommended that the Agency Head ensure that all resources of the local government are safeguarded against loss, wastage, illegal or improper disposition as required by Section 2 of Presidential Decree 1445.

We have recommended that the management be more prudent in purchasing electronic gadgets which should be essential and responsive to the exigencies of the service. We further recommend that management strictly comply with the guidelines on the prevention of unnecessary expenditures as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-003 to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the concerned LGU officials and employees exert extra effort to intensify collection of delinquent accounts and adopt the procedures and remedies provided in Section 254 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991, to enable the collections of unpaid local taxes and employ all necessary measures to enable collection of unpaid stall rentals and electric bills.  These will provide additional funds to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its constituents.
We have recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on withholding and remittance of taxes.

We have recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of GSIS premiums.
We have recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of PhilHealth contributions.
We have recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions.

We further recommend that the other members of the council be encouraged to participate in the preparation of Local Development Plan and Local Investment Plan for Children and other reports in order to have more accurate basis in determining the needs of children and other children related issues that needs immediate attention and action.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the chairman of the MDRRMC, ensure that the LDRRMF are used to support disaster risk reduction and management projects and activities in compliance with the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and other pertinent provisions of related laws and its implementing rules and regulations in order to strengthen the capacity of the LGU to build the disaster resilience of communities, institutionalize measures for reducing disaster risks and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels.

To address the concern on inadequacy of personnel, management may consider adopting an application system which will provide automated solution for administration of local taxes.
observations.managementAction: Management adopted/installed an application system during the calendar year 2016 which will provide automated solution for administration of local taxes.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that management be more cautious in the implementation of infrastructure projects and strictly comply with the submission of documentary requirements in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations to protect the interest of the local government unit at all times.

For the foregoing deficiencies, we have further recommended that the concerned officials and employees submit the supporting documents being required for further evaluation of the Audit Team and in order to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit. The total deficiencies totaling P16,620.01 in the concreting of Labit-Tombod farm to market road should also be refunded. 

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure the completeness of supporting documents before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management caused the refund of the deficiencies totaling P16,620.01 in the concreting of Labit-Tombod farm to market road.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee, who has the responsibility for ensuring that the procuring entity abides by the standards set forth by RA 9184 and its IRR, strictly adhere with all provisions of laws, rules and regulations pertaining to procurements to protect at all times the interest of the local government unit. For the foregoing deficiencies noted, we have recommended that the local government unit  –

Observe the procedural guidelines under GPPB Resolution No. 09-2009 dated November 23, 2009 in all procurements through shopping and small-value procurement to ensure that the most advantageous prices for the government are obtained;

Post the necessary warranty security in accordance with Section 62 of RA 9184 and its IRR for projects funded by the National Government and implemented by the Local Government Unit by Administration; and 

Adhere with the existing guidelines on the manual labor component of projects undertaken by administration and submit supporting documents showing compliance thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management adopted the pakyaw system on labor requirements for projects implemented by administration.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts through the use of complete subsidiary ledger for the operation of the drying facility to facilitate the preparation of financial reports of the economic enterprise as well as the evaluation of its operations. Relative thereto, the collection of fees from the operation of the Drying Facility be included in the Revenue Code and a separate account be maintained for the special account.
observations.managementAction: Management proposed a certain amount from their savings which will serve as the initial capital for the operation of the Drying Facility, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan and Sangguniang Panlalawigan.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 100 and 272 which govern the disposition of the Special Education Fund and refrain from spending government funds for private purposes in compliance with the provisions of Section 335 of the Local Government Code. We have further recommended that the concerned government officials and employees submit supporting documents to support the legality of the claims in order to avoid refund of the amount involved.
observations.managementAction: Documents such as certification of the Schools Division Superintendent that no DepED fund was expended in relation to the Team Building Trip to Hongkong, pictures and unsigned/unapproved Matrix of Activities were submitted by management.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that management refrain from granting gasoline allowance to officials who are receiving transportation allowance. Considering that the officials concerned already received transportation allowance at the maximum rate prescribed by the existing laws, rules and regulations we have further recommended that the gasoline allowance received in the total amount of P631,150.00 be refunded immediately in order to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management informed the concerned officials and was able to make partial refund of the amount involved.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the local government unit refrain from paying the fuel consumption of private vehicles in compliance with the provisions of Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.  If it is confirmed that the motor vehicles provided in the schedule are privately-owned, the local government unit require the owners of these vehicles and/or the persons responsible for providing fuel to these vehicles to pay the government the equivalent value of the fuel cost.
observations.managementAction: Management has committed to comply with the given recommendations and assured the Audit Team that this will no longer be repeated in the future.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to use five percent of the sum of the Estimated Direct Cost, Overhead, Contingencies and Miscellaneous (OCM) and Contractor’s Profit (CP) as VAT component in the computation of indirect costs of infrastructure projects in compliance with the provisions of Department Order No. 22 dated February 18, 2015 of DPWH.

Since the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) is the ceiling for acceptable bid prices, we have further recommended that the guidelines provided in the same Department Order of the DPWH in the preparation of ABC be strictly observed.
observations.managementAction: Five percent VAT component was used in the computation of indirect costs of infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the local government unit refrain from allowing the Consultant/s to collect overtime pays in compliance with the provisions of Budget Circular No. 10 dated March 29, 1996 and require the concerned Consultant to refund the overtime pays received. Strict compliance with the provisions of existing laws, rules and regulations on the rendition and payment of overtime services was further recommended.

Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that management require the concerned officials and employees to comply with the stated regulations for the proper reporting and utilization of gasoline, oil and lubricants. We have further recommended that management require the submission of Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption and duly accomplished and serially numbered Driver’s Trip Ticket for each trip for control purposes and to prevent the wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditure for fuel consumption of government vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Unit submitted Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption and Driver’s Trip Ticket for travels undertaken

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that claims of Representation and Transportation Allowances be supported with the required documents in order to have a basis in determining whether the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations were observed in granting RATA. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer see to it that the claims for RATA are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment in order to avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions.

Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact his collections with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations to avoid the risk of loss or improper utilization of government funds.

Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the Treasury Unit submit the Report of Collections and Deposits together with the duplicates of the official receipts which shall be the basis of the Accounting Unit in the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher and recording in the Cash Receipts Journal in compliance with the provisions of Sections 29 and 35 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System and also to facilitate the verification of the accuracy of the RCD and determination of the timeliness of remittance of collections.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Unit submitted the Report of Collections and Deposits together with the duplicates of the official receipts.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the concerned personnel exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit and rendering of the required reports in compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to render accounts on time during the year.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that management refrain from maintaining controlled dumpsite and prioritize the operation of the Material Recovery Facility (MRF) in compliance with the provisions of RA 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program since the availability and operation of the MRF is indispensable to achieve sound ecological solid waste management and to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the local government unit store and handle the construction materials in a manner to prevent loss, damage and deterioration of materials and prioritize adopting proper measures for its possible disposal or to arrive at an outcome advantageous to the local government unit.
observations.managementAction: The construction materials were stored in the motor pool/bodega near the VLTC.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the management ensure that the projects/programs designed under the 20% Development Fund are implemented fully within the timeframe. Optimal utilization of the 20% of the IRA was also recommended in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents which is essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.
observations.managementAction: Funds appropriated for CY 2016 20% Local Development Fund totaling P2,560,000.00 was not utilized for the period while some economic development projects costing P4,685,296.60 were not completed within the target completion date, thus, reiterated in Observation No. 24, page 80.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the concerned LGU officials and employees exert extra effort to intensify collection of delinquent accounts and adopt the procedures and remedies provided in Section 254 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991, to enable the collections of unpaid local taxes and employ necessary measures to enable collection of unpaid stall rentals.  These will provide additional funds to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its constituents.

Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on withholding and remittance of taxes.
observations.managementAction: Remittance of taxes withheld were made on time.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of GSIS premiums.
observations.managementAction: GSIS premiums were remitted on time.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the local government unit to continue formulate their annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget (GPBs), ensure gender mainstreaming in their policies, programs and projects and comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on Gender and Development to guarantee the promotion of public welfare services addressing gender issues.
observations.managementAction: The Gender and Development Plan and Budget (GPB) for Calendar Year 2016 of the local government unit was submitted and reviewed by the Regional Director of DILG, Regional Office No. 1, City of San Fernando, La Union for consistency, appropriateness and responsiveness of programs and projects to address the gender issues.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the concerned officials and employees of the local government unit submit a written explanation regarding the implementation of the disaster response activities, which in this case, recovery program to farmers stricken by Typhoon Lando was made through the Villasis Municipal Government Employees Multi-Purpose Cooperative (VMGEMPC) and submit documents to support the legality of the transfer of all of the balance of the prior years’ QRF and the current year’s QRF totaling P7,000,000.00 to the same cooperative. A fund utilization report be also required from the concerned cooperative which will show the amount received, expenses incurred and ending balance of the fund and submit a copy of the report to the Audit Team. 

We have further recommended that the provisions of existing laws, rules and regulations relative to the utilization of LDRRMF and transfer of government funds to NGOs be strictly observed.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted documents supporting the disbursements made.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

We have recommended that the local government unit require the Municipal Accountant and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit the monthly sources and utilization of the LDRRMF in compliance with the Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. | AAR 2015
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Unit submitted report on sources and utilization of calamity fund.
